# Transcrição: 56. Trait 55 - Great Salespeople never stop learning.

- **Fonte:** 56. Trait 55 - Great Salespeople never stop learning.
- **Duração:** 01:21
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:21]

The greatest of salespeople just don't stop learning. They're readers. They listen to audio. They're going to conventions. They're going to conferences. They look for bad books and bad workshops to go to and bad audio downloads. They don't care. You know why? Because the greats are committed to greatness. And even a bad idea, a bad concept, something old and outdated becomes fodder, it becomes food for them. It becomes something they consume and get some gristle off of. See, even when you read something or listen to something or hear somebody in a seminar or workshop and you disagree with it, well, what are you disagreeing with? You're disagreeing with and the mind's going to fill it in with something you do agree with. Oh, I don't agree with that, but I agree with this. See, even in that moment, you're learning. Look, look, quit wasting your time with this dead, junky TV that you're watching. Turn that time into learning, reading, studying, practicing. The great salespeople just don't stop learning. The top CEOs in America last year read over 63 books on average. These are the top paid, most successful, at least financially successful CEOs in the country. Read 63 books. How many books did you read? Because great salespeople don't stop reading, don't stop learning, and when you do, you won't be great anymore.
